82.value: Retire at 45 (financial freedom), life expectancy 80-85, target retirement corpus ~11 crore INR
82.source: Client: 'I think let's make it 45 there.' ... 'life expectancy should be at least 80.' Advisor: 'I will add in another five years. Here we'll take it as 85.' ... 'what we are considering the current requirement, what we have, right, our current expenses, what we are incurring about 1.3 lakh rupees... What you should be working towards accumulating is to lose to around 11cr.'

83.value: 1. Emergency reserve (7 lakh INR, 6-8 months expenses); 2. Marriage expenses (low priority, not in next 5 years); 3. Car purchase (in 2 years, 10 lakh INR budget, 3 lakh down payment); 4. International vacations (3-4 lakh INR/year); 5. Personal skill upgradation/education (20-30 lakh INR in 4 years, may use property or loan/parental help); 6. Short career break (1 quarter, ~3.5 lakh INR, planned after Q3 2026)
83.source: Multiple: Emergency reserve: 'somewhere close to around 6.5 lakh rupees is what we see.' ... 'Can we just to be on the safer side, pull it to seven.' Marriage: 'Marriage expenses. For me it could become a priority. Yes. But it's not in the near future at all. Like I don't think for the next five years.' Car: 'Maybe we can look at a car purchase in like say two years. Maybe we can allocate something around 10 for it.' International vacation: 'International vacations, Yes. I would say around 3 to 4 lakhs per year.' Education: 'I do want to save as much as possible for an education just to keep that possibility open for myself.' ... 'say four years for now.' ... 'I would at least like to save around 20 for a 20 to 30 and then rest. I would definitely take assistance from my parents for it if required parents.' Career break: 'I would want to account for is a short career break if I want to take it.' ... 'Say one quarter, I would say.' ... 'not before Q3, 20, 26.'
